initial_posts.narrative: Based on the policy context, the government has committed over S$500 million for AI R&D through AI Singapore, which I take as the total funding for NAIS 2.0. The co-funding cap per 100E project is explicitly stated as S$330,000. For compute, the policy mentions investment in infrastructure but no specific GPU hours; given the scale of the programme and typical allocations for AI experimentation, I estimate 500,000 GPU hours as a reasonable initial allocation to support multiple projects and training. For trainers, the policy emphasizes upskilling and the AI Apprenticeship Programme, but does not specify a number; based on the need to guide enterprises and practitioners, I estimate 50 AI experts and trainers, which aligns with the size of a national programme.

initial_posts.narrative: As an AI researcher who has led multiple 100E projects, I reason from the inputs and activities to outputs. With S$500 million funding and a co-funding cap of S$330,000 per project, the programme can support roughly 100 projects in 2023, given that each project typically requires co-funding and the cap limits the number. The 500,000 GPU hours and 50 trainers enable substantial prototype development and training. From my experience, about 80% of approved projects successfully build a prototype within the year, so I estimate 80 prototypes. The 30 training workshops, each training around 500 participants, yield about 15,000 trainees enrolled, but completion rates in such programmes are around 30-40% due to the rigorous 9-month commitment, so I estimate 5,000 completers. Enterprises with a solution: many projects involve multiple enterprises, but some enterprises may have multiple solutions; I estimate 60 unique enterprises benefit from at least one solution, based on typical pairing ratios from my past projects. I am the first to post, so no prior posts to address.
evidence: The S$500 million funding and S$330,000 co-funding cap imply roughly 100 projects can be funded in 2023, as each project uses about S$330,000 in co-funding and additional resources.
